description: Build the initial spanning tree bookkeeping (parent, thread, succNum, lastSucc, arcDir, pi) on a state already populated with arcs; define the public initState wrapper.
commit: feat: add initial spanning tree construction and initState entry point
steps: Extend solver_state.go with `func initTree(s *state)`. Must be called after populateArcs.

Build the star tree rooted at s.root: parent[s.root] = -1, pred[s.root] = -1. For each real node i in [0, s.n): parent[i] = s.root, pred[i] = s.arcCount + i. arcDir for artificials was set in populateArcs.

Build thread preorder: thread[s.root] = 0; for i in [0, s.n-1): thread[i] = i+1; thread[s.n-1] = s.root. revThread is the inverse permutation. succNum[s.root] = s.n + 1; succNum[i] = 1 for each real i. lastSucc[s.root] = s.n - 1; lastSucc[i] = i for each real i.

Initialize potentials so every artificial arc has reduced cost 0: pi[s.root] = 0. For each real i: if arcDir[s.arcCount+i] == dirDown (child->parent, i.e. source=i, target=root): reduced cost = arcCost - pi[i] + pi[root] = 0 => pi[i] = arcCost[s.arcCount+i]. If dirUp (root->i): reduced cost = arcCost - pi[root] + pi[i] = 0 => pi[i] = -arcCost[s.arcCount+i].

Define the public entry point `func initState(arcs []Arc, n, src, sink int) (*state, error)` that composes: s := newEmptyState(n, len(arcs)); if err := populateArcs(s, arcs, src, sink); err != nil { return nil, err }; initTree(s); return s, nil.

In solver_state_test.go add `TestInitTree_Structure`: build a 3-node graph state via newEmptyState + populateArcs, call initTree; assert parent array, thread is a valid preorder permutation (every index in [0, totalNodes) appears exactly once), succNum[root]==totalNodes==4, and reducedCost(s.arcCount+i) == 0 for every artificial i.
Add `TestInitState_EndToEnd`: call initState on a 3-node graph; assert s != nil, err == nil, s.n==3, s.root==3, tree invariants as in TestInitTree_Structure.
acceptance_criteria: solver_state.go defines `func initTree(s *state)` and `func initState(arcs []Arc, n, src, sink int) (*state, error)`.
After initState, thread pointers form a valid preorder permutation of [0, totalNodes).
After initState, every artificial arc has reducedCost == 0.
